page: Amazon Web Services r7a.metal-48xl is a memory-optimized bare-metal server powered by AMD EPYC 9R14 processors. Utilizing the x86_64 architecture, this instance features 192 dedicated physical cores and 192 vCPUs operating at a clock speed of 3.7 GHz. The hardware configuration includes 1536.0 GB of high-speed DDR5 memory running at 5 GHz, providing 8.0 GB of RAM per core. No local storage or GPUs are bundled with this server, but it features a baseline network bandwidth of 50 Gbps.

In benchmark evaluations, the r7a.metal-48xl demonstrates top-tier performance across multiple compute and memory metrics. Its multi-core capabilities are highlighted by top-tier results in stress-ng multi-core CPU tests, PassMark CPU scores, and Geekbench multi-core benchmarks. Memory bandwidth is exceptionally high, reaching 25488.86 GB/sec for cached small blocks and over 652 GB/sec for large blocks. The server also excels in high-throughput scenarios, achieving top-tier results in Redis SET operations and static web serving. However, a notable performance tradeoff is observed in LLM inference text generation for small models, which registers in the bottom 25%, despite top-tier prompt processing speeds across small, medium, and large models.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the r7a.metal-48xl offers a high-density resource profile suited for enterprise-scale workloads that require massive memory capacity and dedicated CPU resources without virtualization overhead. The absence of local storage means storage requirements must be met via network-attached options, which is a key architectural consideration. This server is highly suited for memory-intensive workloads, including large-scale in-memory databases, high-throughput web serving, software compilation, and complex scientific simulations.
description: Amazon Web Services r7a.metal-48xl is a memory-optimized bare-metal server equipped with 192 dedicated AMD EPYC 9R14 physical cores operating at 3.7 GHz. It features 1536.0 GB of DDR5 memory running at 5 GHz, delivering 8.0 GB of RAM per core, alongside a baseline network bandwidth of 50 Gbps. This instance does not include local storage or GPUs. Benchmark data indicates top-tier performance in multi-core CPU execution, memory bandwidth, and Redis operations, though text generation speeds for small LLM models represent a performance tradeoff. Given its massive memory footprint and high-speed memory bandwidth, the r7a.metal-48xl is highly suited for in-memory databases, large-scale software compilation, high-throughput web serving, and memory-intensive enterprise workloads.
og_description: Amazon Web Services r7a.metal-48xl is a bare-metal server with 192 AMD EPYC cores, 1536 GB of DDR5 RAM, and 50 Gbps network bandwidth, optimized for memory-intensive enterprise workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services r7a.metal-48xl is a bare-metal server featuring 192 AMD EPYC cores and 1536 GB of DDR5 RAM for memory-intensive workloads.
tagline: A high-density bare-metal server combining massive DDR5 memory capacity with dedicated AMD EPYC processing power for memory-intensive enterprise applications.
bullet_points: Powered by 192 dedicated AMD EPYC 9R14 physical cores at 3.7 GHz
Equipped with 1536.0 GB of high-speed 5 GHz DDR5 memory
Delivers 50 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with no local storage
Top-tier multi-core CPU and memory bandwidth benchmark performance
Best suited for in-memory databases, web serving, and software compilation
